items.title: At KL Festival 2026, Hannah Yeoh says Malaysians must participate to keep Dataran Merdeka alive
items.summary: Hannah said this in her speech at the closing ceremony of Kuala Lumpur Festival 2026 and the launch of the Asian Music Games (AMG) Kuala Lumpur 2026 at Dataran Merdeka here tonight. She said heritage buildings alone could not bring a city to life. Hannah expressed hope that Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) and Think City would continue organising programmes and events at Dataran Merdeka in the coming weeks to encourage greater public participation.

items.title: Ringgit to remain range-bound next week amid focus on US jobs data
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit is forecast to trade within a narrow range of RM3.95 to RM3.97 against the US dollar next week as trading activity may be subdued during the holiday-shortened week. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid told Bernama that the focus next week would be on the United States labour market, particularly the Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) report and unemployment rate. On a Friday-to-Friday basis, the ringgit ended higher at 3.9625/9670 against the US dollar, compared with 3.9655/9700 a week earlier.

items.title: Pejuang denies receiving Hamzah's application to join party
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Parti Pejuang Tanah Air (Pejuang) has denied receiving an application from sacked Bersatu deputy president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin to join the party. Its president, Datuk Seri Mukhriz Mahathir, also denied that negotiations had taken place between Pejuang and Hamzah's camp to cooperate. Hamzah's political position remains a question mark after he was sacked from Bersatu on Feb 13 for allegedly violating the party's constitution.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia likely to trade range-bound in holiday-shortened week
items.summary: On the index board, the FBM Emas Index decreased by 126.11 points to 12,580.12, the FBMT 100 Index declined by 129.22 points to 12,417.54, the FBM Emas Shariah Index slipped 40.10 points to 12,604.07, while the FBM Mid 70 Index increased 186.03 points to 18,484.33, and the FBM ACE Index rose 37.84 points to 4,763.81. Weekly turnover slipped to 14.77 billion units valued at RM21.55 billion from 18.19 billion units valued at RM17.65 billion a week earlier. The Main Market volume narrowed to 9.13 billion units valued at RM20.32 billion from 9.49 billion units valued at RM15.69 billion previously.

items.title: Murky discharge was temporary measure, says Perhentian Mariott
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A luxury resort on Terengganu's Perhentian Island said the discharge of murky liquid into the sea captured in a viral video was a "temporary measure" undertaken while its wastewater treatment plant was being upgraded. Perhentian Marriott Resort & Spa said wastewater is normally treated before being discharged into the sea. Yesterday, videos showing murky liquid being discharged from a pipe beneath a jetty went viral, drawing criticism from social media users.

items.title: Cops nab bus driver with 29 outstanding summonses after three-vehicle pile-up on Genting road; six people hurt
items.summary: BENTONG, May 31 — A 55-year-old bus driver with a history of 29 traffic offences was detained after he allegedly drove recklessly and caused a three-vehicle crash at KM16 on Jalan Genting Highlands yesterday. The accident occurred at 5.09pm when the driver's tour bus lost control at a bend, crashing into a Perodua Aruz before ramming a second tour bus, several local news outlets reported. The first tour bus driver's urine test came back negative.

items.title: Malaysia open to buying weapons from countries willing to transfer technology, says defence minister
items.summary: SINGAPORE, May 31 — Malaysia will consider weaponry systems procurement from any countries that are willing to share and transfer their technologies, said Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin. “When we do our procurement, we look into countries that can really transfer their technology to us, countries that are willing to not only make us as their customers but look to us as partners. Meanwhile, in response to United States (US) Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth’s call for allied countries to boost their defence spending instead of relying on the US, Mohamed Khaled said Malaysia would continue to chart its defence policy according to its own priorities and pace.

items.title: Three workers escape blaze after abandoned tanker explodes at Kemaman jetty
items.summary: CHUKAI, May 31 — Three workers narrowly escaped death Friday afternoon when an abandoned ship exploded and caught fire while docked at the Glorious Innovation Jetty in Kampung Bukit Kuang, near Kemaman. The vessel had been found adrift without a crew near an oil platform in Terengganu waters last year. “Suddenly, a small explosion occurred, and the ship caught fire.

items.title: 'Where is China' ask delegates at Asian defence forum
items.summary: THE big question hanging over this year's Shangri-La Dialogue, Asia's premier defence forum, is: "Where is China?" For the second year running, Chinese Defence Minister Dong Jun has given the free-wheeling Singapore security meeting a miss, skipping opportunities to meet US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth as well as counterparts from Australia, France, Britain, Japan and other nations. A highlight of the dialogue's annual programme has been a robust speech by China's defence minister or senior official laying out Beijing's defence doctrine and outlook on global tensions.

items.title: Nik Nazmi claims several PH, Umno MPs keen to join Bersama
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) leader has claimed that several members of parliament from Pakatan Harapan and Umno have expressed interest in joining the party led by Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli. Former Setiawangsa MP Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad said the party had advised the MPs to wait until the dissolution of Parliament to avoid potential implications, including the disruption of government stability. Nik Nazmi broke down the remaining membership as follows: 29 per cent are from PKR; 6.3 per cent from Umno; 5.2 per cent from Pas; 2.5 per cent each from Amanah and Bersatu; 2.2 per cent from Muda and 1.3 per cent from DAP.

items.title: More than 400 detained after violence erupts during PSG title celebrations
items.summary: PARIS: Police on Saturday detained more than 280 people in Paris after violent clashes erupted when thousands poured onto the streets during Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League final victory. Some 22,000 police were deployed across France for the game, including 8,000 in Paris, after unrest marred PSG's win in the competition last year. According to the French interior ministry, 416 people were detained nationwide, including 283 who were apprehended in Paris.

items.title: Lebanon PM denounces Israeli campaign as fresh strikes hit south
items.summary: BEIRUT: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel on Saturday of pursuing a "scorched-earth policy" in his country's south, urging a halt to the fighting as Israel carried out fresh airstrikes and issued evacuation warnings for more than a dozen locations. This will bring "neither security nor stability" to Israel, he said. Hizbollah said it launched multiple attacks targeting northern Israel Saturday, and had also clashed with Israeli soldiers in southern Lebanon.

items.title: Meteor travelling at 120,000kph explodes over US, shaking homes across region
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A meteor crashing toward Earth exploded over the northeastern United States on Saturday, NASA said, setting off booms that echoed over the region with a blast equivalent to 300 tons of TNT. The fireball broke up over northeastern Massachusetts and southeastern New Hampshire at 2.06pm (1806 GMT), the US space agency's deputy news chief Jennifer Dooren told AFP in a statement. "The energy released at breakup is estimated to be equivalent to about 300 tons of TNT, which accounts for the loud booms."

items.title: US warns it is ready to resume Iran war as deal remains unresolved
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States warned on Saturday it was "more than capable" of resuming its war with Iran after President Donald Trump said any peace deal must adhere to his red lines, including Teheran never being able to develop nuclear weapons. Iran's IRNA state news agency said air defences shot down a drone "belonging to the US-Zionist aggressor enemy" on Saturday, citing the army. Trump said his priorities for any deal included Iran agreeing to never develop nuclear weapons and the re-opening of the blockaded Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Online Safety Act 2025 a leap forward in protecting children
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: New safeguards to protect children from harmful online content will officially come into effect tomorrow, under the Online Safety Act 2025 (Onsa), marking a significant step in Malaysia's efforts to strengthen digital safety for minors. Onsa was passed to address rising concerns over cyberbullying, online exploitation, harmful content and emerging digital threats affecting internet users. The codes place greater responsibility on online service providers and platforms to reduce digital harms, particularly those involving children and vulnerable users.

items.title: AI deepens online threats to children [WATCH]
items.summary: Experts warn that AI has supercharged online threats to children, as harmful content can be designed to evade moderation algorithms. Sarah Yong Li Hsien, chairperson of the AI, Cyberlaw and Data Protection Committee at the Malaysian Bar, said the problem stems from the widespread availability of content designed to evade scrutiny. She said Malaysia's existing laws offer some protection, but they have limits.

items.title: Same devotion, different dates: Why some countries observe Wesak on different days — and how Malaysian Buddhists celebrate it
items.summary: Wesak Day marks his birth, enlightenment and passing and is revered as the holiest day in Buddhism. Wesak Day commemorates the birth, enlightenment and passing of Gautama Buddha, and is regarded by Buddhists as a major holy day. Don said Wesak Day is also referred to as Waisak, Buddha Purnima, Buddha Jayanthi or Buddha Day in some countries.

items.title: Dozens arrested in Paris clashes on PSG victory night
items.summary: PARIS: Police on Saturday arrested dozens of people in clashes that erupted in the French capital as thousands poured onto the streets during Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League final victory. Some 22,000 police were deployed across France for the game, including 8,000 in Paris, after unrest marred PSG's win in the competition last year. A police officer was also injured.

items.title: Teenager dies after motorcycle collides with car in Klang
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A teenage boy, 18, was killed in an accident involving two cars and a motorcycle along Jalan Kebun, Klang, here yesterday morning. Klang South district police chief, Assistant Commissioner Lim Jit Huey, said the impact caused the victim to be thrown into the opposite lane, where he was then hit by another car that was unable to avoid him. Lim said both car drivers involved were unhurt.

items.title: Queen's recent trip marks latest chapter in close Malaysia–Türkiye relations
items.summary: According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar's Facebook page, the close relationship between Malaysia and Türkiye has seen strong cooperation in defence, trade and international Islamic diplomacy. The relationship between the state of Johor and Türkiye has its own unique history. The statement also said that British influence was applied to the governance and administration system of Johor as a result of Sultan Abu Bakar's close friendship with Queen Victoria.

items.title: KL Festival 2026 concludes, Asian Music Games to take centre stage in September
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Jalan Raja came alive with marching band performances as the Kuala Lumpur Festival 2026 drew to a spectacular close. The evening also marked the official launch of the Asian Music Games (AMG) Kuala Lumpur 2026, scheduled from Sept 4 to 13, which will bring together marching bands, orchestras and music performers from across Asia in an Olympic-style competition format. Also in attendance were Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Fadlun Mak Ujud, Think City senior director Izan Satrina Mohd Sallehuddin and Asian Marching Band Confederation (AMBC) president Sehat Kurniawan Saiman.

items.title: Legacy of Himalaya's 'Sherlock Holmes' lives on in digital
items.summary: IN a crowded Kathmandu restaurant far from Nepal's famed icy peaks, Billi Bierling questions climbers about their ascents, preserving each triumph in the Himalayan Database, mountaineering's revered 60-year-old register of success. German climber Bierling, 58, inherited the stewardship of the archive from her mentor, the late Elizabeth Hawley, an American journalist who began the post-expedition interviews in Nepal while covering an American Everest expedition in 1963. "She never climbed," Bierling said.

items.title: UN places Israel and Russia on sexual violence blacklist
items.summary: NEW YORK: The United Nations on Friday added Israel and Russia to a UN blacklist of countries suspected of committing sexual violence in conflict zones, a move that prompted Israel's foreign ministry to say it would sever all ties with UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres. Guterres' annual report to the UN Security Council on conflict-related sexual violence goes a step further than last year, when he put Israel and Russia "on notice" that they could be added to the list of parties "credibly suspected of committing or being responsible for patterns of rape or other forms of sexual violence." "Violations consisted of rape, including with objects, gang rape, attempted rape, physical violence to the genitals, instances of targeted shooting of the genitals, touching of breasts and genitals, strip and cavity searches conducted without apparent security justification, forced nudity and threats of rape," it said.

items.title: Singaporean man injured in fall at rocky section of Gunung Datuk
items.summary: SEREMBAN: A Singaporean man was injured after allegedly falling at a rocky section while descending Gunung Datuk in Rembau near here this morning. Kota Fire and Rescue Station chief, Senior Fire Officer II Asnizam Kamberon, said the department received a call regarding the incident involving the 52-year-old victim at 10.29am. "The victim was among 11 hikers taking part in a climb to the summit of Gunung Datuk, accompanied by two Negri Sembilan Forestry Mountain Guides.

items.title: Genting crash: Bus driver with 29 traffic summonses arrested over reckless driving
items.summary: BENTONG: A 55-year-old bus driver, who has 29 outstanding traffic summonses, has been arrested for alleged reckless driving that led to a three-vehicle crash at Km16 of the Genting Highlands Road here today. Bentong district police chief, Superintendent Zaiham Mohd Kahar, said police received a report on the incident at 5.09pm involving two express buses and a Perodua Aruz MPV travelling towards the capital. "Due to the impact, the second express bus lost control, skidded and overturned on the left side of the road, while the first bus veered to the right side of the road," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Poison? More artists flee Trump-linked US anniversary concerts
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump's troubled bid for a star-studded musical celebration of the 250th anniversary of US independence took yet another hit Friday after three more acts pulled out citing political tensions. Trump's administration had publicised multiple headliners for a "Freedom 250" concert series between June 25 and July 10 on the National Mall in Washington. On Friday, glam rocker Bret Michaels and country star Martina McBride joined the Commodores and two other groups announcing they would not take part.

items.title: Chow extends condolences to family of man killed in Odeon building fire
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow has extended his deepest condolences to the family of a man who died in a fire at the Midnight Black entertainment outlet, formerly the Odeon Theatre, along Penang Road here tonight. Chow said the Fire and Rescue Department had confirmed one fatality in the incident as of tonight. Chow also expressed appreciation to firefighters and emergency responders for their professionalism and prompt action in containing the fire and safeguarding nearby residents and properties.

items.title: KL Festival 2026 concludes, Asian Music Games to take centre stage in September
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Jalan Raja came alive with marching band performances as the Kuala Lumpur Festival 2026 drew to a spectacular close. The evening also marked the official launch of the Asian Music Games (AMG) Kuala Lumpur 2026, scheduled from Sept 4 to 13, which will bring together marching bands, orchestras and music performers from across Asia in an Olympic-style competition format. Also in attendance were Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Fadlun Mak Ujud, Think City senior director Izan Satrina Mohd Sallehuddin and Asian Marching Band Confederation (AMBC) president Sehat Kurniawan Saiman.

items.title: One killed in fire at Penang's historic Odeon building
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: One person was killed in a fire that broke out at the former Odeon Cinema building along Penang Road here tonight. Penang Fire and Rescue Department director Mohamad Shoki Hamzah confirmed that one person had died, believed to have been trapped in the blaze. Meanwhile, the Penang Fire and Rescue Department's operations centre said it received an emergency call at 8.50pm.

items.title: What if the AI boom goes into reverse?
items.summary: Annual investment in technology equipment and software reached US$1.5 trillion last year — about 70 per cent more than during the inflation-adjusted peak of the late-1990s dotcom boom. In a mild correction of around 4.5 per cent, US equities could fall roughly 15 per cent, while European markets could drop more than 20 per cent. In a typical tech recession with a six per cent investment decline, losses could exceed 20 per cent in the US and 30 per cent in Europe, potentially triggering broader economic recession as investors repatriate capital.

items.title: Investigative journalism is indispensable
items.summary: IN an era marked by complex financial crimes, abuse of authority and institutional corruption, investigative journalism has become more important than ever. Through objective, professional reporting, investigative journalists help drive action from authorities and encourage transparency in government insti-tutions. One of the most influential examples of investigative journalism was the 2016 Panama Papers investigation coordinated by the International Consortium of Investigative Journalists.

items.title: NST Leader: Are our rail ambitions on track?
items.summary: INVESTIGATORS looking into the Ampang/Sri Petaling light rail transit (LRT) train derailment near the Chan Sow Lin station on Thursday, will likely point to one of three vulnerabilities: human error, mechanical ageing (the LRT is almost 30 years old) or software glitches. Besides the recent derailment, currently blamed on a faulty track switch, there have been several major incidents. In 2008, a train rear-ended another train, resulting in minor injuries among the 300 passengers.

items.title: DOSH orders work halt after Indonesian worker killed in wall collapse
items.summary: audreymd@nst.com.my BUKIT MERTAJAM: The Penang Department of Occupational Safety and Health (DOSH) has ordered an immediate halt to all work activities at the site where an Indonesian worker was killed after being crushed by a wall at Jalan Guar Perahu Indah here yesterday. The order follows the completion of preliminary investigations into the incident. "We are investigating a fatal workplace accident involving a male worker who is believed to have been crushed by a wall while carrying out demolition works at a premises undergoing renovation.

items.title: City council penalises driver caught on video littering at Magazine Road Ghaut
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang Island City council (MBPP) has issued a compound notice to the owner of a vehicle caught on video allegedly throwing rubbish from a moving car at Magazine Road Ghaut. On May 29, MBPP said it became aware of a video circulating on Facebook showing the act, which was uploaded by China Press. It said a compound notice was issued to the vehicle owner for the offence of disposing of rubbish from a vehicle at Magazine Road Ghaut.

items.title: Fire damages historic Odeon building in George Town
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A fire broke out at the former Odeon Cinema building along Penang Road tonight, sending firefighters rushing to contain the blaze at one of the state's most recognisable heritage landmarks. The building, which housed the Midnight Black nightclub, was engulfed in flames in the 8.30pm incident. The affected building occupies the site of the historic Odeon Cinema, one of Penang's most storied entertainment landmarks.

items.title: Malaysia labels Israeli move to take over 70pc of Gaza as evil and unacceptable
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — Malaysia has expressed utter condemnation for a recently announced plan by the Zionist Israeli regime to seize and expand its control over 70 per cent of the Gaza Strip. The ministry, also known as Wisma Putra, said that Gaza has long been an integral part of the Palestinian Territory even before the creation of Israel, and any move by the regime to alter Gaza’s territorial reality will lead to further blatant injustices and exacerbate the already volatile peace in the region. “The people of Gaza continue to suffer because of the Zionist Israeli regime’s genocide and destruction in Gaza.

items.title: Malaysia pivots to ‘tourism plus X’ as it courts China’s luxury and youth travellers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — Malaysia is intensifying its tourism promotion efforts in China by targeting high-end, youth and education-based travel segments to capture the changing preferences of Chinese consumers, Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing said. In his Facebook posting today, Tiong said Tourism Malaysia has been actively engaging with key players in China’s travel industry, particularly in Shanghai, as part of efforts to tap deeper into the growing demand for luxury, youth-oriented and education-focused travel experiences. During a recent outreach mission to Shanghai, Tiong said a Tourism Malaysia delegation led by Tourism Malaysia Director-General Mohd Amirul Rizal Abdul Rahim met with major Chinese tourism industry players, including LeYou Group, luxury travel specialist 8 Continents Travel and Cuttlefish Travel, to explore emerging market trends and potential areas of collaboration.

items.title: Wildlife camera footage confirms missing hiker Jaslinda was heading to Sukaneka Camp; search narrows
items.summary: IPOH, May 30 — Missing female hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, has been confirmed to be heading towards the Sukaneka Camp at Gunung Batu Putih, near Tapah, based on important clues from wildlife surveillance camera footage obtained today. “There is a recording showing the victim (Jaslinda) crossing Camera 1 at 11.22 am on May 24, 2026. “For Camera 2, there are no recordings of the victim or the rescue team passing by Camera 2 heading towards Gunung Batu Bujang on May 24 and 25.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms across half of Malaysia tonight
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds affecting six states and Labuan until 9pm today. In Sarawak, similar conditions are expected in Kuching, Serian, Samarahan, Sri Aman, Betong, Kapit and Bukit Mabong, Bintulu, Subis, Beluru, Miri and Marudi in the Miri Division and Limbang. In Sabah, the affected areas are expected to include the Interior (Sipitang, Kuala Penyu, Beaufort and Nabawan), the West Coast, Tawau (Tawau), Sandakan and Kudat.

items.title: Nine times more likely than adults: CAP warns Malaysia is losing a generation to vaping
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 30 — Despite various health awareness campaigns conducted every year, the use of nicotine or tobacco products such as vapes, electronic cigarettes and conventional cigarettes among children and teenagers continues to raise concern. Consumers Association of Penang (CAP) education officer N.V. Subbarow said the situation must be taken seriously as the country now faces the risk of producing a generation trapped in nicotine addiction at an increasingly young age due to the growing number of children and teenagers using vapes and electronic cigarettes. In conjunction with World No Tobacco Day 2026, which will be observed tomorrow, he said Malaysia must take bold action by banning all nicotine products, including cigarettes, vapes, electronic cigarettes, heated tobacco products and nicotine pouches, to protect the younger generation from increasingly alarming addiction.

items.title: RM100 per head: Malaysian driver caught smuggling nine Bangladeshis in Myvi after dramatic skid and crash in Tumpat
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 30 — A 21-year-old local man and nine Bangladeshi immigrants were detained after the car they were travelling in skidded off the road before crashing into a vehicle and a house in Kampung Tok Malik, Wakaf Bharu, Tumpat, today. General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade Commander, SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the incident, which occurred at about 7 am, took place when a team from the GOF 7th Battalion Task Force was conducting an Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan patrol in the area. Checks found that the vehicle was driven by a local man believed to be acting as a land transporter and was carrying nine Bangladeshi immigrants,” he said when contacted today.

items.title: Heading out for Wesak Day in Penang tomorrow? Plan your route, skip the firecrackers
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 30 — The Penang police have prohibited the public attending the Wesak Day celebration and procession tomorrow from playing with firecrackers and fireworks, said the state police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail. He said the public is also advised to plan their journeys earlier and use alternative routes to avoid getting caught in traffic congestion during the Wesak Day celebration. The procession route involves Jalan Burmah (start), Jalan Pangkor, Jalan Kelawai, Lorong Burmah, Jalan Burmah, Jalan Perak (against the flow of traffic), Lebuhraya Peel (against traffic flow), Jalan Macalister (against the flow), Jalan Perak (against the flow), Jalan Anson (against the flow), Jalan Macalister, Lorong Madras, and back to Jalan Burmah.

items.title: Aiming for safer trails, UPSI plans to set up mountaineering school
items.summary: IPOH, May 30 — Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI) is planning to establish a Mountaineering School to help stimulate the hiking industry while minimising incidents in mountainous areas. UPSI Vice-Chancellor Prof Datuk Dr Md Amin Md Taff said the idea for such a school emerged from discussions with the President of the Malaysian Mountain Guides Association and the Fire and Rescue Department of Malaysia, who highlighted the need for a training and research centre focused on hiking and mountain-related activities. He said that, in line with the concept of lifelong learning, the school will offer a variety of courses related to the mountain industry, including research skills, hiking, rescue operations, environmental ethics, the socio-economic aspects of the hiking industry, nature interpretation, hiking event management and more.

items.title: Former MACC chief Azam Baki to file defamation suit against businessman Albert Tei next week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — Former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said he will file a defamation suit against businessman Albert Tei next week, following allegations that he threatened and attempted to prevent the disclosure of claims linked to the Sabah corruption scandal. Azam told Astro Awani today that the legal action was intended to defend his reputation and that of the anti-graft agency, while ensuring the public was made aware of the facts surrounding the matter. “The public needs to know the facts.

items.title: ‘A true patriot’: Dzulkefly hails IJN cardiothoracic surgeon Dr Jeswant Dillon’s historic AATS induction
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — Prof Datuk Seri Dr Jeswant Dillon’s induction as the first Malaysian into the American Association for Thoracic Surgery (AATS) represents the highest form of recognition for his excellence, dedication and contributions to the field of cardiothoracic surgery. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad said this achievement was not attained overnight but is the result of the surgeon’s ongoing commitment and years of devoted service in advancing medical care. “On behalf of the Ministry of Health and the government, I extend my deepest respect to Dr Jeswant for this truly well-deserved recognition.

items.title: PKR election director reshuffle is about strategy, not personalities, says Youth chief
items.summary: PADANG RENGAS, May 30 — The change in PKR’s election director post is part of a strategic restructuring exercise aimed at strengthening the party machinery ahead of the next election. On the appointment of PKR vice-president Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari as the party’s election director alongside Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, Muhammad Kamil said both leaders possess extensive experience in managing election operations and organisation. “Previously, Amirudin served as election operations director, giving him the knowledge and experience required.

items.title: Minister says RM1.5b Sabah interim grant shows federal government is listening
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 30 — The increase in the special interim grant for Sabah, from RM600 million to RM1.5 billion, demonstrates the federal government’s ongoing commitment to addressing the state’s rightful claims gradually and responsibly. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak), Datuk Mustapha Sakmud, said the announcement by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim marks a significant achievement, reflecting the Madani Government’s determination to ensure that Sabah’s interests continue to receive due attention. Mustapha said the issue of Sabah's 40 per cent revenue entitlement has dragged on for decades.

items.title: Anwar says government cuts its own spending to channel more aid to rakyat
items.summary: PENAMPANG, May 30 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the Madani Government has implemented strict cost-cutting measures across ministries, with the savings fully redirected towards programmes and initiatives that benefit the people. He said allocations for ministries would be reduced for non-essential spending, although critical sectors such as education, healthcare and basic infrastructure, including in Sabah, would be spared. Anwar said the cost-cutting measures include limiting ministers’ overseas trips, except for mandatory meetings under international platforms such as Asean, Brics and Apec.

items.title: Raja of Perlis launches Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Free Trade Zone-Perlis Inland Port
items.summary: PADANG BESAR, May 30 — The Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, today graced and officiated the launch of the Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Free Trade Zone-Perlis Inland Port (PIP) here. In his royal address, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin said the development of PIP as a free trade zone was not merely a symbol of physical development but a strategic step towards strengthening Perlis’ and Malaysia’s position as a regional trade and logistics gateway, anchored on intra-Asia land transportation, particularly the Pan-Asia railway network. Also gracing the event was the Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail.

items.title: Loke: Railway cable theft is a national security issue, govt eyeing tougher laws, tech solutions
items.summary: Transport Minister Anthony Loke said his ministry is looking into the use of technology to detect and prevent cable theft activities from recurring, as these thefts are a major issue for the service, particularly in the northern region. Loke said his ministry is also discussing with the Home Ministry (KDN) about tightening the laws against cable theft because, despite actions being taken regarding these cases, they are still rampant. Loke said that his team is also examining the practices and appropriate solutions used by other countries to address the same issue, considering that cable theft cases also occur in countries with rail systems.

items.title: Cops: Man found dead in locked car outside Penang ice cream shop died of carbon monoxide poisoning
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 30 — A man found dead inside a car parked outside an ice cream shop along Jalan Sungai Dua here yesterday was confirmed to have died from carbon monoxide poisoning. Northeast District deputy police chief Superintendent Lee Swee Sake said the cause of death of the 37-year-old man was determined following a post-mortem examination conducted at the Forensic Department of Penang Hospital today. “The victim is also believed to have died about five days before his body was discovered yesterday.

items.title: Rescuers retrieve trail camera data in search for missing hiker Jaslinda on Gunung Batu Putih
items.summary: TAPAH, May 30 — A team has been deployed to retrieve surveillance camera chips around the Gunung Batu Putih hiking trail here to monitor whether a female hiker reported missing for the past week was detected passing through the area. Perak Fire and Rescue Department assistant operations director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the task was jointly undertaken by a Protection Team from the National Parks and Wildlife Department (Perhilitan) and 16 rescue personnel. “The Protection Team, together with the rescue team, will retrieve surveillance camera chips around the trail area to monitor whether the victim can be detected through the surveillance camera route.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues thunderstorm and heavy rain warning for KL, five states until 5pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department has issued a thunderstorm, heavy rain and strong winds warning for five states and Kuala Lumpur until 5pm today. The department in a statement said the affected areas in Pahang are Bentong and Bera, while in Selangor the warning covers Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat. In Sabah, the affected areas are in Ranau, Kunak, Lahad Datu, Telupid, Kinabatangan, Beluran, Sandakan, Kota Marudu and Pitas.

items.title: Sabah steps up efforts to position Kaamatan as an international tourism attraction, says DCM
items.summary: PENAMPANG, May 30 — Sabah is intensifying efforts to promote the Kaamatan Festival as an international tourism product through strategic collaboration with various agencies and industry players to showcase the state’s unique cultural heritage on the global stage. Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Seri Joachim Gunsalam said the initiative is being implemented through cooperation between the State Government, Sabah Tourism Board, Ministry of Tourism, Arts and Culture and the Kadazandusun Cultural Association (KDCA). Dr Joachim, who is also the State-Level Kaamatan Festival organising chairman, said that through the strategic measures, Kaamatan is not only celebrated as the cultural heritage of Sabah’s people but is also promoted as a premier tourism product capable of attracting more domestic and international visitors.

items.title: Anwar says Sabah’s growth has surpassed projections, KKIA upgrade to be expedited
items.summary: PENAMPANG, May 30 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said rapid growth in Sabah’s development and tourism sector has surpassed earlier projections, prompting the federal government to expedite infrastructure upgrading works, particularly at Kota Kinabalu International Airport (KKIA). However, Sabah’s growth has surpassed normal projections and recorded development that is truly commendable,” he said when launching the state-level Kaamatan Festival 2026 at Hongkod Koisaan, Kadazandusun Cultural Association (KDCA) here today. Also present were Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Seri Dr Joachim Gunsalam, and KDCA president Tan Sri Joseph Pairin Kitingan.

items.title: End of the road for Aeroline in KL after bus operator loses Corus and TRX footholds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — Aeroline is set to pull out of Kuala Lumpur entirely, ending more than two decades of operations in the city after losing access to two key locations that had served as gateways for its premium coach services. In a statement on Facebook today, the bus operator said Kuala Lumpur would no longer serve as a landing point for its coaches, adding that regulatory requirements now restrict operations to licensed terminals. However, Aeroline said it received a directive on May 13 requiring it to operate only from licensed terminals.

items.title: Perlis Inland Port set to boost Malaysia-Thailand trade and regional connectivity, says Loke
items.summary: Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the close ties between Malaysia and Thailand have long been the foundation of economic development in the border areas of both countries, and the PIP would further strengthen these economic relations through more efficient, faster and integrated cross-border trade facilitation. PIP has the potential to become a key land trade gateway linking ASEAN countries with broader markets in East Asia and Eurasia,” he said. He said this at the opening ceremony of the Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Free Commercial Zone at PIP today, officiated by the Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail.

items.title: Unity minister calls for wiser digital culture in Wesak Day message
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang has called on Malaysians to uphold human values, strengthen unity and progress towards a more ethical, well-mannered and wise digital society. “In today’s digital world, unity is tested not only in the real world but also on social media. (While) a single post can unite society, a slander can destroy national harmony,” he said in a Facebook post today.

items.title: Malaysia backs regional push for stronger military cooperation, joint drills at Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore
items.summary: SINGAPORE, May 30— Asean defence ministers today agreed to step up joint military training and exercises, particularly in humanitarian assistance and disaster relief operations. According to Singapore’s Defence Ministry (Mindef), the ministers exchanged views on expanding cooperation through initiatives such as Indonesia’s Exercise Trident Resolve, scheduled for September 2026 and involving Asean Defence Ministers’ Meeting Plus (ADMM-Plus) countries, as well as enhanced information-sharing through platforms such as the Republic of Singapore Navy’s Information Fusion Centre. The meeting was co-hosted by Singapore Defence Minister Chan Chun Sing and Philippines Secretary of National Defence Gilberto Teodoro Jr, and attended by defence leaders and representatives from Asean member states.

items.title: PM Anwar’s Aidiladha contribution benefits 2,000 Orang Asli residents in Ulu Kinta
items.summary: Tambun parliamentary special officer Mohamad Hairul Amir Sabri said the programme was organised by the Tambun Parliamentary Community Service Centre in collaboration with the Department of Orang Asli Development (JAKOA), with the participation of the Orang Asli community. The programme involved Orang Asli community from Kampung Makmur, Kampung Tonggang, Kampung Chadak, Kampung Suluh, Kampung Suak Petai, Kampung Choh and Kampung Baduk. Meanwhile, Kampung Makmur Tok Batin Jailani Rasid said the programme benefited the Orang Asli community while strengthening ties among residents.
